---
title: "I can't read NTFS FILES"
date: 2007-11-20
forum: Absolute Beginner Talk
---

### Post by dalvarez on 2007-11-20
I installed ntfs-3g in my UBUNTU 7.10 Gutsy, but i can't copy files to ext3 partitions, or play mp3 music in NTFS Windows partition.

The FSTAB file is,

```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda4 :
UUID=b5f965da-f339-45ea-8c52-450a4e3c4cba / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda2 :
UUID=f930e651-02a2-479e-b879-6e60c881d0a3 /boot ext3 defaults 0 2
# /dev/sda1 /media/sda8 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=es_CR.UTF-8 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda3 :
UUID=77f1a1af-d55c-469f-b0a0-9a04153ebf04 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
/dev/fd0 /media/floppy0 auto rw,user,noauto 0 0
/dev/sda1 /media/windows ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.utf8 0 0

```

The devices mounted are:

```
$ mount -l
/dev/sda4 on / type ext3 (rw,errors=remount-ro) []
proc on /proc type proc (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev)
/sys on /sys type sysfs (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev)
varrun on /var/run type tmpfs (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev,mode=0755)
varlock on /var/lock type tmpfs (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev,mode=1777)
udev on /dev type tmpfs (rw,mode=0755)
devshm on /dev/shm type tmpfs (rw)
devpts on /dev/pts type devpts (rw,gid=5,mode=620)
lrm on /lib/modules/2.6.22-14-generic/volatile type tmpfs (rw)
/dev/sda2 on /boot type ext3 (rw) []
securityfs on /sys/kernel/security type securityfs (rw)
binfmt_misc on /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc type binfmt_misc (rw,noexec,nosuid,nodev)
/dev/sdb1 on /media/disk type vfat (rw,nosuid,nodev,shortname=mixed,uid=1000,utf8,umask=077,usefree) []
[COLOR="Red"]/dev/sda1 on /media/windows type fuseblk (rw,nosuid,nodev,noatime,allow_other,blksize=4096)[/COLOR] []

```

The error message when copy any file from NTFS to ext3 for example is:

```
$ cp 01\ -\ Shredded\ Humans.mp3 $HOME
cp: no se puede abrir `01 - Shredded Humans.mp3' para lectura: Permiso denegado
```

Now, rename the file that is in NTFS partition /media/windows...

```
 $ mv 01\ -\ Shredded\ Humans.mp3 01.mp3
$ ls -l 01*
-rwxrwxrwx 1 root root 12460690 2007-09-26 15:40 01.mp3
```

No problem the 'mv' command, then I try to copy again

```
$ cp 01.mp3 $HOME
cp: no se puede abrir `01.mp3' para lectura: Permiso denegado
```


Please i need support, thanks.

### Post by szaka on 2007-11-21
What is the full output of

```

strace cp 01.mp3 $HOME

```

---

### Post by lespaul_rentals on 2007-11-21
First, you need to create a mount point in the *mnt* folder.  Name it "windows" or something like that.  Now, assuming the device name is "sda1" and the mount point is "windows", run this command as user:

```
ntfs-3g /dev/sda1 /mnt/windows
```

You may have to add the **-f** tag to the command for it to mount.

This will allow you to read/write data to the NTFS drive.  To unmount it, run this command as super user:

```
sudo fusermount -u /mnt/windows
```

---

### Post by Dark_X on 2007-11-22
> **dalvarez said:**
> I installed ntfs-3g in my UBUNTU 7.10 Gutsy,

Why would you do that when it is installed by default.  That might be why you can't copy files.

### Post by szaka on 2007-11-23
Thanks but unfortunately you did a completely else, wrong command then what I have asked.

Please repeat exactly this if you still have the '01 - Medieval.mp3' file. Note the apostrops!
```

strace cp '01 - Medieval.mp3' $HOME

```

---

### Post by szaka on 2007-11-24
The file is quite probably encrypted. Transparent decryption is not yet supported by the driver, sorry. You should decrypt it under Windows.

---

### Post by dalvarez on 2007-11-24
OK, now I can cp NTFS files. I unencrypted the files.

Many Thanks

---
